reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff (March 15, 2026), there was unanimity among economists and market participants that the Federal Reserve would not change the target federal funds rate at its March 18, 2026 meeting. A Reuters poll of 96 economists conducted between March 6 and March 12 unanimously predicted a hold. Market probability on March 6th was already at 0.99, and the news out to the middle of March reflects the market pushing back expectations of any rate cuts into June or September due to inflation and labor market stability. Therefore, a ‘no change’ outcome is overwhelmingly likely. I predict 0.95, keeping within the required confidence boundaries.
